summary: See clues file for full details. 7 tossups, 9 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 7 tossups and 9 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: In one poem, one of these people declares "your soft hand is of a woman itself" before exclaiming "you smile?" that's what "[these people] call our harmony." The question "why can't [one of these people] lift each foot in turn" appears in another poem where one of these people claims his business is to do one action to the "souls of men" and not flesh.
examples: In one poem, one of these people declares "your soft hand is of a woman itself" before exclaiming "you smile?" that's what "[these people] call our harmony." The question "why can't [one of these people] lift each foot in turn" appears in another poem where one of these people claims his business is to do one action to the "souls of men" and not flesh.

clues.clue: The claim (*) "man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's a heaven for?" appears in a poem titled for one of these people that is addressed to Lucrezia.
examples: The claim (*) "man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's a heaven for?" appears in a poem titled for one of these people that is addressed to Lucrezia.

clues.clue: The Duke of Ferrara describes a creation by one of these people named Fra Pandolf in another poem.
examples: The Duke of Ferrara describes a creation by one of these people named Fra Pandolf in another poem.

clues.clue: Fra Lippo Lippi and Andrea del Sarto held, for 10 points, what occupation during the Renaissance?
examples: Fra Lippo Lippi and Andrea del Sarto held, for 10 points, what occupation during the Renaissance?

clues.clue: This character laments that he "was tempted and complied / and built this house and sinned," before telling a woman "you loved me quite enough, it seems tonight.
examples: This character laments that he "was tempted and complied / and built this house and sinned," before telling a woman "you loved me quite enough, it seems tonight.

clues.clue: This must suffice me here." This character confesses that because "less is more," a "truer light of God" burns in his rivals' "vexed beating stuffed and stopped-up brain." He compares himself to a "weak-eyed bat no sun should tempt out of the grange whose four walls make his world" while pleading for more time with his (*) wife, who is being called away by "the Cousin's whistle." This character tells his wife Lucrezia that had she "given [him] soul," they "might have risen to Rafael." While criticizing his own passionless precision, he argues "Ah, but a man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's a heaven for?" For 10 points, name this speaker of a dramatic monologue by Robert Browning, an artist known as the "faultless painter."
examples: This must suffice me here." This character confesses that because "less is more," a "truer light of God" burns in his rivals' "vexed beating stuffed and stopped-up brain." He compares himself to a "weak-eyed bat no sun should tempt out of the grange whose four walls make his world" while pleading for more time with his (*) wife, who is being called away by "the Cousin's whistle." This character tells his wife Lucrezia that had she "given [him] soul," they "might have risen to Rafael." While criticizing his own passionless precision, he argues "Ah, but a man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's a heaven for?" For 10 points, name this speaker of a dramatic monologue by Robert Browning, an artist known as the "faultless painter."

clues.clue: This poem's speaker complains about those of turbulent temperament by saying: "The sudden blood of these men! at a word - / Praise them, it boils, or blame them, it boils too." The speaker of this poem recalls "kingly days" when "Too live the life grew, golden and not grey," and repeatedly expresses a desire to retreat to "sober pleasant Fiesole." This poem praises the "...perfect brow / And perfect eyes, and more than perfect mouth" of a woman, but laments that she cannot inspire beyond her beauty, saying "Had you, with these the same, but brought a (*) mind!" This poem's narrator meditates on the success of rivals who access "a heaven that's shut to [him]," and in whom "there burns a truer light." This poem's final line of "Again, the Cousin's whistle!" references the ongoing infidelity of its speaker's wife, Lucrezia.
examples: This poem's speaker complains about those of turbulent temperament by saying: "The sudden blood of these men! at a word - / Praise them, it boils, or blame them, it boils too." The speaker of this poem recalls "kingly days" when "Too live the life grew, golden and not grey," and repeatedly expresses a desire to retreat to "sober pleasant Fiesole." This poem praises the "...perfect brow / And perfect eyes, and more than perfect mouth" of a woman, but laments that she cannot inspire beyond her beauty, saying "Had you, with these the same, but brought a (*) mind!" This poem's narrator meditates on the success of rivals who access "a heaven that's shut to [him]," and in whom "there burns a truer light." This poem's final line of "Again, the Cousin's whistle!" references the ongoing infidelity of its speaker's wife, Lucrezia.

clues.clue: This artist included a giraffe in a fresco which he made for the Medici Villa at Poggia a Caiano called The Triumph of Caesar, which was finished by his fellow Florentine artist Alessandro Allori.
examples: This artist included a giraffe in a fresco which he made for the Medici Villa at Poggia a Caiano called The Triumph of Caesar, which was finished by his fellow Florentine artist Alessandro Allori.

clues.clue: A man with well-defined buttocks drops a severed head in one of this artist's grisaille frescoes on the life of John the Baptist that he made for the Cloister of the Scalzco.
examples: A man with well-defined buttocks drops a severed head in one of this artist's grisaille frescoes on the life of John the Baptist that he made for the Cloister of the Scalzco.

clues.clue: His version of the Last Supper contains a representation of the Trinity above two servants who stand on a balcony overlooking the central scene.
examples: His version of the Last Supper contains a representation of the Trinity above two servants who stand on a balcony overlooking the central scene.

clues.clue: Putti hold slabs from Mary's tomb as they surround a rising Mary in a painting this artist created that was partly based on his Passerini Assumption.
examples: Putti hold slabs from Mary's tomb as they surround a rising Mary in a painting this artist created that was partly based on his Passerini Assumption.

clues.clue: The most famous work of this artist of the Panciatichi Assumption was originally supposed to contain St.
examples: The most famous work of this artist of the Panciatichi Assumption was originally supposed to contain St.
